title: First Snow
summary: A child from a tropical country visits a colder place for the first time and sees snow. She describes the wonder of touching it, the biting cold, and her total surprise at this magical white world.
body: I grew up in a hot country where it never snows. Last winter, I visited my aunt in Canada. One morning, I woke up and looked out of the window. Everything outside was completely white. I could not believe my eyes. It was snow! I had seen snow in pictures and movies, but never in real life. I put on my coat, hat, and gloves and ran outside. The first thing I noticed was the cold. It was much colder than I expected. My breath made little clouds of steam in the air. I bent down and touched the snow with my fingers. It was soft and fluffy, a little like cotton wool, but very cold and wet. I picked up a handful and it melted quickly in my warm hands. The garden was so quiet. The snow covered everything — the grass, the trees, and the garden chairs. I tried to walk but my feet sank into the soft snow with each step. I laughed out loud because it felt so strange and wonderful. My aunt came outside and showed me how to make a snowball. I threw it at the fence and it broke into pieces. It was one of the most amazing mornings of my life.

annotations.exampleEn: She grew up by the sea and loves swimming.

annotations.meaningVi: không thể tin vào mắt mình, quá ngạc nhiên
annotations.exampleEn: When she saw the surprise party, she could not believe her eyes.
She put on her gloves before going out in the cold.
In cold weather, you can see your breath in the air.

annotations.meaningVi: hơi nước (có thể nhìn thấy trong không khí lạnh)
annotations.exampleEn: Steam rose from the hot cup of tea.
The nurse cleaned the wound with a piece of cotton wool.
He threw a handful of seeds onto the soil.
The ice cream melted quickly in the hot sun.
His boots sank into the mud with every step.
The children threw snowballs at each other in the park.
The vase fell and broke into pieces on the floor.
The test was harder than I expected.

grammarNotes.title: Past Simple – Telling a Story of a First Experience
grammarNotes.bodyVi: Toàn bộ bài này dùng thì quá khứ đơn để kể lại một trải nghiệm đáng nhớ. Hãy chú ý các động từ bất quy tắc: grow → grew, wake → woke, see → saw, bend → bent, pick → picked (thông thường), melt → melted, sink → sank, come → came, throw → threw, break → broke. Kể lại câu chuyện trong quá khứ là một kỹ năng rất thực tế.
grammarNotes.bodyEn: This passage uses the simple past throughout to tell a memorable first-time story. Notice these irregular verbs: grow → grew, wake → woke, see → saw, bend → bent, sink → sank, come → came, throw → threw, break → broke. Telling personal stories in the past is one of the most useful conversational skills in English.

grammarNotes.title: Comparisons with 'much + comparative'
grammarNotes.bodyVi: 'Much' được dùng để nhấn mạnh sự so sánh — nghĩa là 'nhiều hơn rất nhiều'. Ví dụ: 'It was much colder than I expected.' (Lạnh hơn nhiều so với tôi nghĩ.) Không dùng 'very colder' — sai. Luôn dùng 'much colder', 'much better', 'much harder', v.v.
grammarNotes.bodyEn: 'Much' intensifies a comparative adjective — it means 'a lot more'. Example: 'It was much colder than I expected.' Do NOT say 'very colder' — that is incorrect. Always say 'much colder', 'much better', 'much harder'. The structure is: much + adjective-er + than.

translationVi: Tôi lớn lên ở một đất nước nóng bức, nơi không bao giờ có tuyết. Mùa đông năm ngoái, tôi đến thăm dì ở Canada. Một buổi sáng, tôi thức dậy và nhìn ra cửa sổ. Mọi thứ bên ngoài đều màu trắng tinh. Tôi không thể tin vào mắt mình. Đó là tuyết! Tôi đã từng thấy tuyết trong ảnh và phim, nhưng chưa bao giờ thấy thật. Tôi mặc áo khoác, đội mũ, đeo găng tay và chạy ra ngoài. Điều đầu tiên tôi cảm nhận là cái lạnh. Lạnh hơn nhiều so với tôi tưởng. Hơi thở của tôi tạo ra những đám hơi nước nhỏ trong không khí. Tôi cúi xuống và chạm vào tuyết bằng các ngón tay. Tuyết mềm và xốp, giống như bông gòn, nhưng rất lạnh và ướt. Tôi cầm một nắm tuyết và nó tan chảy nhanh chóng trong đôi bàn tay ấm của tôi. Khu vườn rất yên tĩnh. Tuyết phủ lên tất cả mọi thứ — cỏ, cây cối và những chiếc ghế trong vườn. Tôi cố gắng bước đi nhưng chân tôi lún xuống tuyết mềm theo từng bước. Tôi cười to vì cảm giác thật kỳ lạ và tuyệt vời. Dì tôi ra ngoài và chỉ cho tôi cách làm một viên tuyết tròn. Tôi ném nó vào hàng rào và nó vỡ ra từng mảnh. Đó là một trong những buổi sáng tuyệt vời nhất trong cuộc đời tôi.

questions.prompt: Where did the narrator grow up?
questions.explanation: The story says 'I grew up in a hot country where it never snows.'
questions.hint: What does the narrator say about her home country's weather?

questions.prompt: Where did she visit in winter?
questions.explanation: The story says 'I visited my aunt in Canada.'
questions.hint: Which country did she travel to?

questions.prompt: What did she see when she looked out of the window?
questions.explanation: The story says 'Everything outside was completely white.'

questions.prompt: Had she ever seen real snow before?
questions.explanation: The story says 'I had seen snow in pictures and movies, but never in real life.'
questions.hint: Where had she only seen snow before this day?

questions.prompt: What three things did she put on before going outside?
questions.explanation: The story says 'I put on my coat, hat, and gloves.'
questions.hint: Name the three items of clothing she wore.

questions.prompt: What did her breath look like in the cold air?
questions.explanation: The story says 'My breath made little clouds of steam in the air.'
questions.hint: What could she see coming from her mouth in the cold?

questions.prompt: What does she compare the snow's texture to?
questions.explanation: The story says 'a little like cotton wool, but very cold and wet.'
questions.hint: What soft material does she use to describe the snow's feel?

questions.prompt: What happened when she held the snow in her hands?
questions.explanation: The story says 'it melted quickly in my warm hands.'
questions.hint: What does warmth do to snow?

questions.prompt: What happened to her feet when she tried to walk?
options: Her feet sank into the soft snow
questions.explanation: The story says 'my feet sank into the soft snow with each step.'
questions.hint: What did the snow do when she stepped on it?

questions.prompt: Who showed her how to make a snowball?
questions.explanation: The story says 'My aunt came outside and showed me how to make a snowball.'
questions.hint: Which family member came out to help her?

questions.prompt: What happened when she threw the snowball at the fence?
questions.explanation: The story says 'it broke into pieces.'
questions.hint: What did the snowball do when it hit the fence?

cloze.template: I grew up in a hot country where it never {{b1}}. Last winter, I visited my aunt in Canada. One morning, I woke up and looked out of the window. Everything outside was completely {{b2}}. I could not believe my eyes. It was snow! I had seen snow in pictures and movies, but never in real life. I put on my coat, hat, and {{b3}} and ran outside. The first thing I noticed was the cold. It was much colder than I {{b4}}. My breath made little clouds of {{b5}} in the air. I bent down and touched the snow with my fingers. It was soft and {{b6}}, a little like cotton wool, but very cold and wet. I picked up a handful and it {{b7}} quickly in my warm hands. The garden was so quiet. The snow covered everything — the grass, the trees, and the garden chairs. I tried to walk but my feet sank into the soft snow with each step. I laughed out loud because it felt so strange and {{b8}}. My aunt came outside and showed me how to make a {{b9}}. I threw it at the fence and it broke into {{b10}}. It was one of the most amazing mornings of my life.

blanks.explanation: 'It never snows' means snow never falls in that country — because it is too hot.
'Completely white' describes a landscape fully covered by fresh snow.
'Gloves' are worn on the hands to protect against cold — essential in snowy weather.
'Much colder than I expected' means the reality was far colder than her prediction.
'Little clouds of steam' describes how warm breath becomes visible vapour in very cold air.
'Soft and fluffy' describes fresh snow — light, airy, and not packed down.
'Melted' means the snow turned from solid to liquid because of the warmth of her hands.
'Strange and wonderful' perfectly captures the feeling of experiencing something new and magical for the first time.
A 'snowball' is a ball made by pressing snow together in your hands — perfect for throwing.
'Broke into pieces' means the snowball shattered apart when it hit the hard fence.

criticalThinkingQuestion: The narrator had seen snow many times in photos and films, but nothing prepared her for the real experience. Why do you think actually experiencing something is so different from just seeing or reading about it? Can you think of an example from your own life?
